# Is Ray, Not Array (English — full index)

> Hi, I'm Ray — a software engineer who refuses to be treated as an Array. Tech notes and hands-on write-ups on web development, from pitfalls to working solutions.

## About
- [Ray](https://israynotarray.com/en/about/): I'm a software engineer and blogger. — author of Is Ray, Not Array
- [Support my work](https://israynotarray.com/en/support/)

## BEVN Network (related properties owned by the same author)
- https://israynotarray.dev — Personal portal site
- https://tools.israynotarray.dev — Online utilities collection (100+ tools)

- https://gems.israynotarray.dev — AI gems collection

- https://line-analysis.israynotarray.dev — LINE sticker analysis tool
- https://calculator.israynotarray.dev — Calculator collection: 19 everyday life and finance calculators

### Gulp (22)
- [Fixing "regeneratorRuntime is not defined" When Using Gulp](https://israynotarray.com/en/gulp/2022/11/18/gulp-babel-regeneratorruntime-not-defined-fix/): A quick note on fixing the `regeneratorRuntime is not defined` issue when using gulp-babel.

This post is just a quick note on how to fix it.
- [Gulp Series Bonus: Deploying Gulp to Heroku](https://israynotarray.com/en/gulp/2019/12/13/gulp-series-20-heroku-deploy/): Haven't updated this series in a while. I recently needed to deploy my Gulp setup to Heroku, so let me write it up as a bonus chapter.

- [Gulp Series: Watch](https://israynotarray.com/en/gulp/2019/06/13/gulp-series-14-watch/): We've got browser-sync for local dev — but do we have to restart it every time we change a file? Nope. Gulp has a `watch` API that monitors file changes and re…

- [Gulp Series: Running Tasks in Sequence/Parallel](https://israynotarray.com/en/gulp/2019/06/11/gulp-series-12-parallel-tasks/): As tasks pile up, you'll want a single command to run them all instead of typing gulp scss, gulp babel one by one.
- [Gulp Series: Image Compression](https://israynotarray.com/en/gulp/2019/06/11/gulp-series-11-images/): We've covered HTML, CSS, JS — but no images. Adding images is simple, so this chapter will cover image compression while we're at it.
- [Gulp Series: Minifying JS](https://israynotarray.com/en/gulp/2019/06/11/gulp-series-10-uglify/): Time to add JS minification — only for prod mode. Let me log how it works.

- [Gulp Series: Installing Babel](https://israynotarray.com/en/gulp/2019/06/11/gulp-series-08-babel/): Installing Babel — a tool that compiles ES6 into JavaScript browsers can run.
- [Gulp Series: Simplifying the Gulp Flow](https://israynotarray.com/en/gulp/2019/06/10/gulp-series-07-simplify/): We haven't added Babel and other tools yet, but the `require` list is already getting long. Let me show how to clean it up.
- [Gulp Series: Minifying and Optimizing CSS](https://israynotarray.com/en/gulp/2019/06/10/gulp-series-06-css-minify/): CSS minification was actually already added in the PostCSS chapter. This post is about distinguishing between dev and prod modes.
- [Gulp Series: Installing PostCSS](https://israynotarray.com/en/gulp/2019/06/10/gulp-series-05-postcss/): New chapter — let's install PostCSS to go with the SCSS we set up earlier.

- [(6) Step-by-Step Docker — Common Container Commands (Part 2)](https://israynotarray.com/en/docker/2023/01/26/step-by-step-docker-container-commands-part-2/): Last post covered the basics. This post picks up the remaining commands — restart, ps, exec, logs.
- [(5) Step-by-Step Docker — Common Container Commands](https://israynotarray.com/en/docker/2023/01/25/step-by-step-docker-common-commands/): We covered Docker and Docker Hub commands. Now let's go through the Docker commands you'll actually use day-to-day with containers.
- [(4) Step-by-Step Docker — Docker Hub](https://israynotarray.com/en/docker/2023/01/24/step-by-step-docker-docker-hub/): Now let's talk about another important part of Docker — Docker Hub.
